featured.name: TRIBE v2
featured.tagline: Brain encoding model that predicts fMRI responses across video, audio, and text stimuli.
featured.why_radar: Meta's cracked the code on unified brain encoding across multiple modalities. This isn't just neuroscience research, it's the foundation for building AI that actually understands how humans process information. The implications for brain-computer interfaces and AI alignment are massive.

featured.name: Gemini 3.1 Flash Live
featured.tagline: Real-time multimodal voice model for low-latency audio, video, and tool use.
featured.why_radar: Google's answer to OpenAI's Advanced Voice Mode, but with proper multimodal support and developer API access. The native streaming architecture means no more clunky pipeline setups. This is how voice AI should work.

featured.name: Cohere Transcribe
featured.tagline: State-of-the-art automatic speech recognition model for enterprise speech intelligence.
featured.why_radar: Cohere's moving beyond text generation into speech recognition territory. This signals the consolidation of AI capabilities under single providers rather than fragmented specialist tools. Enterprise customers get one throat to choke instead of managing multiple ASR vendors.

picks.name: Voxtral
picks.tagline: Open-weight text-to-speech model supporting nine languages with voice cloning.
picks.why_radar: Mistral's first TTS model can clone voices from just three seconds of audio across nine languages. The open-weight approach means developers can finally build voice applications without vendor lock-in or usage limits.

picks.name: Wit
picks.tagline: Prevents merge conflicts when multiple AI agents edit the same repository.
picks.why_radar: As AI agents start collaborating on codebases, merge conflicts become a nightmare. Wit solves the coordination problem that nobody saw coming until teams started deploying multiple coding agents simultaneously.

picks.name: SentinelGate
picks.tagline: Open-source MCP proxy providing access control for AI agents.
picks.why_radar: AI agents need proper access controls before they can be trusted in production environments. SentinelGate fills the security gap that MCP left wide open, giving enterprises the guardrails they need.

hn_top5.title: Show HN: I put an AI agent on a $7/month VPS with IRC as its transport layer
hn_top5.summary: Developer built a practical AI agent deployment using IRC as the communication protocol on cheap hosting. Proves you don't need expensive infrastructure to run useful AI agents in production.

hn_top5.title: Show HN: Wit – Stops merge conflicts when multiple AI agents edit the same repo
hn_top5.summary: Tool designed to prevent merge conflicts when multiple AI coding agents work on the same repository. Addresses a coordination problem that's becoming common as teams deploy multiple AI agents.

hn_top5.title: I Built a Lisp for AI Agents
hn_top5.summary: Custom Lisp implementation designed specifically for AI agent workflows. Shows developers are building domain-specific languages to better control AI agent behaviour and reasoning.

hn_top5.title: Show HN: SentinelGate – Access control for AI agents (open-source MCP proxy)
hn_top5.summary: Open-source proxy that adds access controls to Model Context Protocol connections. Fills a critical security gap for enterprises deploying AI agents with external tool access.

hn_top5.title: Ran 100 AI agents through the Community Notes algorithm: the model dominates
hn_top5.summary: Experiment showing how AI agents perform when subjected to Twitter's Community Notes fact-checking system. Results suggest AI models can game collaborative moderation systems at scale.
